weekly newsletter
Week of June 29th
"Getting over a painful experience is much like crossing the monkey bars. You have to let go at some point in order to move forward." - C.S. Lewis

welcome
A note from tim

What a week!

Big thanks to all that came out to the Keto Challenge Launch yesterday. Some great food and excellent conversation! Check the recipe section below for what seemed to be the hit of the party (beside 18 mo Penelope who was decked out in her red checkerboard one piece!)

Quietly sneaking in is Bring a Friend Week! Prizes and reward for those with the most invitations and guests. So many of you have been amazing stewards of promoting and wanting to help grow the gym! Check the steps below for ideas on getting the most out of it!

Additionally, we have a cool new 30 minute class that will be starting July 7th - Buns and Bones!! Danny has been working on some great exercises to help build and strengthen your bones, and now that many of you have actually found your glute muscles, many have mentioned that they want to continue perking the up and giving them some additional attention.

Also, on June 12th, join us for the Summer Sled Series - special sessions on working the sled. Not just HROX class, but for anyone wanting to gain a better understanding of the body dynamics to optimize your sled work (and build those glutes and legs!) This will become a series with more dates announced in the future.

📌 important

Keto participants, make sure to keep up with the emails and the homework as we start crossing off the days of our challenge. It is ONLY 30 days. Don't get left behind!

bring a friend week is here!
here are some ideas to help make bring a friend week more successful!
01
Free Week Vouchers
I will have vouchers for a free week for you to give to those special friends, family and neighbors to help entice them to come!
02
Follow Us!
Be sure to be following us on Instagram and Facebook! Make sure to share it with your friends who are trying to get into the gym. Pick the posts that might resonate with them or address something they are dealing with.

tip teusday

tip teusday
sugar awareness
the sugar trap: 8 easy ways to cut hidden sugars

Most people know to avoid candy and soda - but sugar hides in places you'd never expect. These simple habits make cutting sugar easier without feeling like you're giving up everything.

🏷️
read ingredient labels
Sugar hides under names like dextrose, maltodextrin, corn syrup, cane juice, agave nectar, and rice syrup.
🥤
skip liquid calories
Soda, sweet tea, juice, and flavored coffee drinks can contain more sugar than dessert.
🥩
prioritize protein first
Eating protein at every meal helps reduce cravings and keep you fuller longer.
🛒
shop the perimeter
Fresh meats, produce, eggs, and dairy are usually around the outside of the grocery store.
🍓
use berries when cravings hit
Lower in sugar than most fruits, and often enough to satisfy a sweet tooth.
🥜
keep keto snacks nearby
Nuts, cheese sticks, beef jerky, or hard-boiled eggs can prevent impulse purchases.
💧
drink water before snacking
Thirst is often mistaken for hunger.
😴
protect your sleep
Poor sleep increases hunger hormones and makes sugary foods much harder to resist.

recipe corner
this week's featured recipe
Easy prep
High proteinprep: 15 mincook: 50 minserves: 12
vanilla pound cake

The beauty of this is that it is very hard to mess it up! And I've tried! Make it a lemon pound cake by adding some lemon juice (2 tbs) and grating one lemon into the mixture.

236
cal per serving
24g
total fat
5g
total carbs
2g
Fiber
4g
net carbs
7g
protein
grocery list
— 2 cups superfine almond flour
— 1/2 cup salted butter
— 2 teaspoons of baking powder
— 2 ounces of cream cheese
— 1 cup granulated monk fruit sweetener
— 1 teaspoon vanilla extract
— 1 cup full-fat sour cream
— 4 large eggs
instructions
1
Preheat oven to 350°F. Grease a 9-inch bundt pan.
2
In a large bowl, combine almond flour and baking powder.
3
Melt butter and cream cheese together in a pan over low heat, making sure not to burn the cream cheese.
4
Remove from heat and add monk fruit sweetener, vanilla extract and sour cream, stirring until combined.
5
Slowly mix wet ingredients with dry ingredients.
6
Add eggs and mix until combined.
7
Pour batter into pan and bake for 50 minutes or until a toothpick inserted into the cake comes out clean.
8
Allow cake to cool completely before removing it from the pan.
